description_long: This is a single-bladed axe featuring a broad, curved steel blade with a slightly matte, hammered finish. The blade is attached to a long, gently curved wooden handle, which appears to be made from a dark-stained hardwood. The handle is cylindrical with a consistent thickness and a subtle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The blade has a pronounced cutting edge and a rounded poll, with no visible decorative elements or engravings. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the top of the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks in an urban or outdoor environment.
description: A utilitarian axe with a broad, curved steel blade and a long, dark wooden handle. The design is simple and functional, with a traditional blade mounting.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the side, highlighting the full profile of the curved blade and the ergonomic wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, showing the narrow edge of the blade and the cylindrical handle aligned behind it.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile of the axe, mirroring the blade and handle curvature seen in view 0.
description_view_3: The axe is viewed from the bottom, displaying the straight alignment of the handle and the back of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved cylindrical wooden handle, dark-stained, smooth surface, no visible grip texture, ergonomic curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad curved blade with a pronounced cutting edge, rounded poll, no beard, and a matte hammered finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the top of the handle.
